title: How DesignOps roles are changing with new priorities
description: As AI integrates into design workflows, DesignOps roles are shifting from process management to strategic vision-setting. This article explores how design leaders must evolve their priorities to maintain meaningful human-centered design in an increasingly automated landscape.

title: Your banking app is lying to you
description: Most banking apps show current balances without revealing future implications of spending patterns. This thought-provoking piece examines how AI can transform financial apps from showing snapshots to providing honest, predictive financial guidance that actually serves users.

title: The organizational cost of low taste
description: Organizational failure isn't always about flawed strategy—it's about losing shared standards for quality in daily decisions. This essay reveals how weak design taste compounds into systemic dysfunction and undermines long-term product success.

title: Better search, worse web
description: While AI-powered search engines show impressive metrics, the article argues they come with hidden costs to web ecosystem health and content quality. A critical analysis of how optimizing for search performance alone can degrade the broader value of the internet.

title: After AI takes everything
description: As AI commoditizes technical production, user value will shift from what software can do to how individual users curate and personalize their experience. This forward-thinking piece explores the future of competitive advantage in a post-commoditization world.

title: Show your hands honor for the strange power they bring you
description: Hands are our primary interaction tool, yet designers often overlook their capabilities and limitations in digital interfaces. This thoughtful guide explores designing finger-friendly interactions that honor the nuance and dexterity of human touch.

title: What sits on the engawa
description: Drawing from Japanese architectural philosophy, this essay explores designing for aspirational human desires that users haven't yet articulated. A poetic perspective on anticipating unmet needs and creating space for users to grow into.

title: Deckwise
description: Deckwise introduces an AI presentation agent that generates editable decks, reducing the friction of deck creation while maintaining user control. A practical tool addressing a common pain point in how teams create and iterate on presentations.
